---
title: "MkDocs and GitHub Actions"
subtitle: "Interacting with the DB and Web"
categories: [MkDocs, GitHub Actions, Documentation]
image_path: "../img/mkdocs"
---






## Mkdocs

Mkdocs documents your code and puts it in html format.

### Step 1: Create a Virtual Environment

#### Option 1: Using `venv`

```bash 
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
```
#### Option 2: Using `conda`


```bash  
conda create -n myenv python=3.x
conda activate myenv
```
#### Option 3: Using `uv` (RECOMMENDED)


```bash  
pip install uv
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: .venv\Scripts\activate
```

### Step 2: Install mkdocs

**After activating your virtual environment, you can install `mkdocs` and the necessary plugins using pip:**

```bash
pip install mkdocs-material
pip install "mkdocstrings[python]"
pip install pillow cairosvg
```

### Step 3: File Structure

#### Linux/MacOS

```bash
mkdir -p .github/workflows &&  touch .github/workflows/ci.yaml 
```

#### Windows

```bash
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path ".github/workflows"; New-Item -ItemType File -Force -Path ".github/workflows/ci.yaml"
```

#### Create `Docs` folder and respective `.md` files

1. Create `docs` folder
2. Create respective `.md ` files with proper links


::: {.callout-important title ="EXAMPLE: File Structure"}

```bash
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .github
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ workflows
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ ci.yaml
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ demo.md 
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ etl.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ api.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ app.md
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mkdocs.yaml
```

Example: api.md

```bash
## API

### Main.py

::: myapp.api.main

### Models.py

::: myapp.api.Database.models
```


:::





### Step 4: Create `mkdocs.yaml` and `ci.yaml` files

#### `mkdocs.yaml`


```yaml
site_name: My Docs

nav:
  - Home: index.md
  - Demo: demo.md
  - ETL: etl.md
  - API: api.md
  - APP: app.md


theme:
  name: material
  features:
    - navigation.tabs
    - navigation.sections
    - toc.integrate
    - navigation.top
    - search.suggest
    - search.highlight
    - content.tabs.link
    - content.code.annotation
    - content.code.copy
  language: en
  palette:
    - scheme: default
      toggle:
        icon: material/toggle-switch-off-outline
        name: Switch to dark mode
      primary: teal
      accent: purple
    - scheme: slate
      toggle:
        icon: material/toggle-switch
        name: Switch to light mode
      primary: teal
      accent: lime

plugins:
  - mkdocstrings:
      handlers:
        python:
          paths: ["."]
```


#### `ci.yaml`


```yaml
name: ci

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - master
      - main

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.x"

      - name: Cache Python packages
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('**/requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install MkDocs dependencies
        run: |
          pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install mkdocs-material
          pip install "mkdocstrings[python]"
          pip install pillow cairosvg
      - name: Build MkDocs site
        run: mkdocs build        
      - name: Deploy mkdocs
        run: mkdocs gh-deploy --force
```


### Preview of the documentation

Given that the virtual environment is activated and you are in the **root** directory of your project, you can run the following command to serve the documentation locally:

```bash
mkdocs serve
```



![]({{< meta image_path >}}/mkdocs_main.png)
